Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Can I run AI locally? 1238 upvotes, 306 comments
  2. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 50 upvotes, 7 comments
  3. Baochip-1x: What It Is, Why I'm Doing It Now and How It Came About 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 516 upvotes, 152 comments
  5. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 325 upvotes, 91 comments
  6. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 131 upvotes, 32 comments
  7. Using Thunderbird for RSS 104 upvotes, 29 comments
  8. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 769 upvotes, 299 comments
  9. Hammerspoon 290 upvotes, 105 comments
  10. You gotta think outside the hypercube 65 upvotes, 18 comments
  11. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 29 upvotes, 6 comments
  12. Our Experience with I-Ready 71 upvotes, 25 comments
  13. Recursive Problems Benefit from Recursive Solutions 24 upvotes, 6 comments
  14. Optimizing Content for Agents 48 upvotes, 18 comments
  15. AEP (API Design Standard and Tooling Ecosystem) 17 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe 212 upvotes, 119 comments
  17. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  18. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 106 upvotes, 57 comments
  19. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  20. Emacs and Vim in the Age of AI 152 upvotes, 93 comments
  21. Show HN: Context Gateway – Compress agent context before it hits the LLM 81 upvotes, 49 comments
  22. New 'negative light' technology hides data transfers in plain sight 89 upvotes, 55 comments
  23. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 590 upvotes, 507 comments
  24. Digg is gone again 197 upvotes, 178 comments
  25. Coding after coders: The end of computer programming as we know it? 130 upvotes, 161 comments
  26. Parallels confirms MacBook Neo can run Windows in a virtual machine 273 upvotes, 382 comments
  27. Elon Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters 444 upvotes, 665 comments
  28. I beg you to follow Crocker's Rules, even if you will be rude to me 78 upvotes, 117 comments
  29. Wired headphone sales are exploding 147 upvotes, 249 comments
  30. UCP Protocol: The Internet Has 100M Shops and No Front Door 5 upvotes, 4 comments

Last generated 7:30pm, 14 March 2026. Source here.
